overview: This walkthrough describes 'College Finder', a front-end only college discovery platform built with Next.js 13, React 18, HTMX, and Tailwind CSS v4. It covers architecture, design decisions, trade-offs, and edge case handling. Key features include college listings with search/filters, detailed college pages (sections, placements, reviews), side-by-side comparison of up to three colleges, saving colleges and comparisons, and a full client-side login/signup flow with local storage persistence. The project emphasizes a strict separation between UI primitives and domain components, efficient state management using Zustand, and a thin page component pattern.
discussion_points: Project architecture and component organization (UI vs. Domain components)
State management using Zustand vs. React Context for global persistence
Trade-offs regarding local storage for persistence vs. IndexedDB for larger datasets
Logic encapsulation in hooks (e.g., `useColleges` for filtering, sorting, pagination)
Search debouncing implementation and optimization using `useDebounce` hook
Design and implementation details of the college comparison feature (data mapping, dynamic row rendering)
Handling edge cases like compare bar placeholders and filter page reset
CSS custom properties with Tailwind CSS and the resolution of invalid CSS issues
Accessibility features: modal focus trap, ARIA attributes for buttons (`aria-pressed`, `aria-label`), and `aria-current` for active sections with Intersection Observer.
Trade-offs for a production context: lack of server state management, client-side authentication, and absence of a test suite.

decisions.decision: Separation of UI primitives and domain components
decisions.rationale: Allows changing the entire visual design of a button without touching feature files. UI components are pure and reusable across projects, domain components compose primitives and read from system stores.

decisions.decision: Use of Zustand over React Context for global persistence state (auth, compare, save stores)
decisions.rationale: Zustand allows components to subscribe to specific slices of state, preventing unnecessary re-renders for other subscribers when unrelated values change. Context would cause all subscribers to re-render on any change.

decisions.decision: Use of Zustand's persistent middleware with local storage
decisions.rationale: Automatically serializes state to local storage and re-hydrates on page load, simplifying persistence without manual JSON handling. Suitable for the project's scope.

decisions.decision: Consolidation of all listing page logic into a single hook (`useColleges`)
decisions.rationale: Keeps page components thin, acting as pure coordinators that feed state and process callbacks. Improves maintainability and testability.

decisions.decision: Debouncing search input at 250 milliseconds with a custom `useDebounce` hook
decisions.rationale: Prevents excessive filter computation on every keystroke, ensuring performance scales for larger datasets (e.g., 2000 colleges).

decisions.decision: Using `useMemo` for filter results dependent only on filters, search, and sort key
decisions.rationale: Optimizes performance by recomputing filter results only when relevant dependencies change, avoiding re-computation on view mode or page changes.

decisions.decision: Resetting search to page 1 when filters change
decisions.rationale: Prevents empty grids with no feedback when applying filters that yield fewer results than the current page offset.

decisions.decision: Mapping partial college data for listing page and full data for comparison
decisions.rationale: Listing page loads only necessary data; comparison page needs additional fields (placement rate, average package, etc.). Ensures efficiency.

decisions.decision: Defining comparison data as an array of objects (`rows`) with `label`, `getValue`, and optional `highlight` functions
decisions.rationale: Allows dynamic rendering of comparison tables. Adding new comparison metrics is a simple object addition, requiring no JSX changes. `getBestIDX` function finds winning columns.
Ensures consistency across the UI (e.g., compare bar slots) and prevents hardcoding, fixing bugs where different parts of the UI had conflicting values.

decisions.decision: Using CSS custom properties (`--sapphire`, `--cloud-radius`) referenced by Tailwind's arbitrary values (`bg-[--sapphire]`)
decisions.rationale: Provides a design system with single-source-of-truth for design tokens, ensuring consistent styling.

decisions.decision: Replacing CSS custom property references with concrete Tailwind classes for buttons
decisions.rationale: Reduces indirection for critical interactive elements, ensuring maximum control and least potential for unexpected styling issues.

decisions.decision: Implementing a focus trap for modals
decisions.rationale: Enhances accessibility by restricting keyboard focus within the modal dialogue, preventing screen readers and keyboard users from interacting with elements behind the modal.
Communicates toggle state to assistive technology, indicating that the button changes state rather than just performs an action.

decisions.decision: Using `aria-label` with the full college name for save/compare buttons
decisions.rationale: Provides context to screen readers (e.g., 'Save MIT' instead of just 'Save').

decisions.decision: Using `aria-current` on the active section button on the detail page, driven by an Intersection Observer
decisions.rationale: Highlights the active section for accessibility and user experience. The observer picks the topmost intersecting section to handle fast scrolls.

next_steps: Introduce server state management (e.g., React Query or SWR) for a real product instead of static files.
Migrate authentication persistence from local storage to HTTP-only cookies with server validation for production security.
Add a comprehensive test suite (unit, E2E) leveraging the existing testable architecture (pure functions, clear store interfaces, stateless components).
